name: Gorath the Smith
description: A massive man with arms like tree trunks, working the forge with an artisan's precision. Sweat glistens on his dark skin.

start.text: If you've come to browse, keep your fingers off the blades. If you've come to talk, make it quick — this steel won't shape itself.
start.narrative: Gorath plunges a glowing blade into a water trough, sending up a hiss of steam.

responses.text: What can you tell me about your work?
I need something to fight with.

craft.text: Forty years at the anvil. I've forged swords for kings and horseshoes for farmers. Everything I make, I make to last. No cheap tin work in my forge.
craft.narrative: He examines the blade critically before setting it aside.

weapons.text: I've got blades on the rack and shields on the wall. If you find rare materials — good steel, unusual ore — bring them to me. I can work wonders with the right ingredients.
weapons.narrative: He nods toward the weapon display.

materials.text: The ruins used to be an Eltharan forge-city. If the stories are true, there's star-metal down there — lighter than steel, harder than diamond. Bring me some of that and I'll make you something legendary.
materials.narrative: For the first time, his eyes light up with genuine excitement.

ruins.text: My grandfather told stories of Eltharan smiths who forged blades that sang. The ruins north of here — that was their city once. Something's waking up there. The priestess came to me last week asking about old Eltharan seals.
ruins.narrative: He strokes his chin, leaving a smudge of soot.

seals.text: Magical locks, forged in metal. The Eltharans sealed something beneath their city. The priestess thinks the seal is weakening. That's what those lights are — old magic leaking out.
seals.narrative: He sets down his hammer and looks at you seriously.

responses.text: I should talk to the priestess.
